items.title: Joty and Co aim to finish strong at Lord’s
items.summary: The match is set to begin at 3:30pm (Bangladesh time).

items.title: Australia hold the key as India, South Africa chase semi-final spots
items.summary: South Africa are the favourites to beat Bangladesh, but their poor net run rate means their chances hinge on Australia beating India

items.title: Tigers out to hit top form in one-off Zimbabwe Test
items.summary: Zimbabwe also arrive with confidence after beating Afghanistan in a one-off Test in October last year.

items.title: Habibul backs Amite to seize Test opportunity
items.summary: The Bangladesh chief selector believes Amite Hasan has the technique to thrive in Test cricket and wants him to play without the burden of expectations

items.title: Bangladesh moves to criminalise match-fixing, spot-fixing
items.summary: The proposed Gambling Prevention Bill prescribes up to seven years' imprisonment for fixing offences, with the BCB also seeking greater legal powers for its integrity unit

items.title: India, South Africa eye NRR boost in close Group 1 race
items.summary: South Africa won the only T20I they played against Netherlands in 2010, while India hold a 20-3 head-to-head record against Bangladesh

items.title: Zimbabwe look to build on rare Test momentum against Bangladesh
items.summary: Ngarava begins his captaincy stint as Zimbabwe seek to back up last year's Sylhet win over a confident Bangladesh

items.title: Tigresses’ semifinal hopes extinguished after five-wicket loss to India
items.summary: Bangladesh, with four points, have one remaining match against South Africa

items.title: Fit again Litton Das joins Bangladesh ODI squad for Zimbabwe tour
items.summary: Mehidy Hasan captains a side full of confidence after beating Australia 2-1 recently

items.title: Tigers name unchanged ODI squad for Zimbabwe tour
items.summary: The three ODIs are slated for July 6, 9 and 11 respectively at Harare Sports Club.

items.title: Bangladesh bat, India bring in Radha and Renuka
items.summary: While a loss wouldn't be tournament-ending, it will make qualifying much tougher

items.title: Bangladesh face India in Women's T20 World Cup survival battle
items.summary: Three teams are currently vying for the second qualifying position in Group 1, with India, South Africa, and Bangladesh sitting second, third, and fourth in the standings respectively

items.title: Sikandar Raza to miss Zimbabwe's Test against Bangladesh
items.summary: Richard Ngarava set for captaincy debut in the one-off Test between Zimbabwe and Bangladesh in Harare from June 28
